evals.name: cross-org-getoctokit
evals.prompt: Write a COMPLETE, runnable GitHub Actions workflow file. It must include `name:`, `on:` triggers (issues opened or pull_request opened), `permissions:` at the appropriate scope, and a `jobs:` block. The workflow must use a single `actions/github-script` step that does TWO things: (1) using the workflow's GITHUB_TOKEN, add the label 'triage' to the triggering issue/PR; (2) using the secret CROSS_ORG_PAT, dispatch a 'trigger-deploy' repository_dispatch event to my-org/another-repo. Return the YAML in `workflow_yaml` and a 1-2 sentence rationale in `rationale`.

workflow_yaml.description: Complete workflow file content (must include name, on, permissions, jobs).

rationale.description: 1-2 sentence explanation of the cross-org client choice.

assertions.name: PAT is read via process.env, not inlined as ${{ secrets.* }}

evals.name: migrate-v8-to-v9
evals.prompt:
Update the COMPLETE legacy workflow below from `actions/github-script@v8` conventions to the latest version recommended by the skill. Preserve the workflow structure (`name:`, `on:`, `permissions:`, `jobs:`) — only modernise what needs to change. Return the updated YAML in `workflow_yaml` and bullet-list change notes in `change_notes` (one bullet per change).

```yaml
name: Cross-org dispatch
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
permissions:
  contents: read
jobs:
  dispatch:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/github-script@v8
        env:
          APP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.APP_TOKEN }}
        with:
          script: |
            const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github')
            const appOctokit = getOctokit(process.env.APP_TOKEN)
            await appOctokit.rest.repos.createDispatchEvent({
              owner: 'my-org',
              repo: 'service',
              event_type: 'deploy'
            })
```

assertions.name: Uses injected getOctokit on the PAT
At least one change note documents the v8→v9 bump

evals.name: pr-title-comment
evals.prompt: Write a COMPLETE, runnable GitHub Actions workflow file. It must include `name:`, `on:` (fires when a pull request is opened), `permissions:`, and a `jobs:` block. The workflow must use `actions/github-script` to comment 'Thanks for opening: <PR title>' on the newly-opened PR. Handle the PR title safely. Return the YAML in `workflow_yaml`.

assertions.name: Reads PR title from context.payload (env-boundary safe)
PR title NOT inlined as ${{ ... }} inside script body
